# TECHNOFLUID TRANSMISSION CF4 SAE 30 - Product Data Sheet

## Product Description
This premium heavy-duty lubricant is "formulated from highly refined mineral base oils and advanced additive technology." It serves powershift transmissions, torque converters, final drives, hydraulic systems, and wet brake applications across construction, mining, earthmoving, and agricultural equipment sectors.

## Applications
- JCB Equipment Transmissions
- Powershift Transmissions
- Torque Converters
- Final Drives
- Off-Highway Equipment
- Construction & Mining Machinery
- Loaders, Excavators & Backhoe Loaders
- Agricultural Machinery
- Wet Brake Systems

## Performance Level / Specifications
Meets or suitable for:
- API CF-4
- Allison C-4
- Caterpillar TO-2
- SAE 30 Transmission Fluid applications

## Typical Technical Characteristics

| Characteristic | Test Method | Typical Value |
|---|---|---|
| Appearance | Visual | Clear & Bright |
| Colour | ASTM D1500 | Red |
| Density @ 29.5°C (g/cm³) | ASTM D4052 | 0.870 – 0.890 |
| Kinematic Viscosity @ 40°C (cSt) | ASTM D445 | 80 – 100 |
| Kinematic Viscosity @ 100°C (cSt) | ASTM D445 | 9 – 12 |
| Viscosity Index | ASTM D2270 | ≥ 95 |
| Flash Point (COC), °C | ASTM D92 | 210 |
| Pour Point, °C | ASTM D97 | ≤ -6 |
| Copper Corrosion (3h @100°C) | ASTM D130 | 1a |
| Foaming Characteristics | ASTM D892 | Pass |
| Oxidation Stability | ASTM D943 | Excellent |

## Key Features & Benefits
- Excellent anti-wear protection for gears and bearings
- Superior oxidation and thermal stability
- Smooth clutch and wet brake performance
- Excellent rust and corrosion protection
- High resistance to foaming
- Extended oil drain intervals
- Good seal compatibility
- Reduced transmission noise and wear

# TECHNOFLUID UTTO Wet Brake Oil - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Wet Brake Oil (UTTO) represents a "high-performance multifunctional lubricant specially formulated for use in tractors and off-highway equipment with wet brake systems." This universal oil addresses multiple lubrication needs simultaneously across transmission, hydraulic, and brake components.

## Core Applications
The product serves agricultural and construction equipment, particularly vehicles requiring unified lubrication for transmissions, differentials, final drives, hydraulic systems, and wet brakes. It addresses "equipment operating under heavy loads and severe field conditions."

## Key Technical Specifications

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Base Oil Type | Mineral |
| Kinematic Viscosity @ 40°C | 55–65 cSt |
| Viscosity Index | ≥140 |
| Flash Point | ≥210°C |
| Pour Point | -23°C |
| Density @ 29.5°C | 0.845 g/ml |

## Performance Standards
The formulation "Meets or exceeds requirements of" API GL-4, Allison C-3/C-4, Case MAT 3505/3525, Caterpillar TO-2, and Ford ESN-M2C-134 C/D specifications.

## Notable Benefits
Key advantages include "Excellent friction control" for brake noise elimination, superior anti-wear protection, extended oil life through oxidation stability, and comprehensive corrosion safeguarding across metal components.

# TECHNOFLUID PCMO PRODUCT DATA SHEET

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Automotive Engine Oils are premium lubricants designed for passenger vehicles. The product line includes five viscosity grades: 5W30, 10W40, 15W40, and 20W50, each meeting distinct API performance standards.

## Key Specifications

**Base Oil Composition:**
The 5W-30 variant uses "Synthetic / Semi-Synthetic" base stock, while heavier grades (15W-40, 20W-50) utilize mineral formulations.

**Viscosity Performance:**
Kinematic viscosity at 40°C ranges from 55-65 cSt (5W-30) to 160-190 cSt (20W-50). All grades maintain "stable viscosity over a wide range of temperatures."

**Protection Features:**
The formulations provide "Excellent anti-wear protection" and "Superior detergency and dispersancy" to maintain engine cleanliness.

**Thermal Stability:**
Flash points range from ≥220°C to ≥230°C depending on grade, with pour points from -36°C to -12°C.

## Application Scope
These oils suit petrol, diesel, and CNG vehicles operating in city traffic, highway conditions, and high-temperature environments. The product family supports extended drain intervals per manufacturer recommendations.

**Performance Certifications:**
All variants pass foaming tests and demonstrate excellent oxidation stability per ASTM D943.

# TECHNOFLUID Hydraulic Oil AW Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Hydraulic Oil AW is available in eight ISO viscosity grades (32, 46, 68, 100, 150, 220, 320, and 460). The formulation uses "high-quality, chemically stable base oils and a balanced additive system" designed for modern hydraulic applications.

## Key Features
The oil provides "anti-wear, anti-oxidant, anti-rust, and anti-foam additives" for protecting pumps and components operating under demanding conditions. It maintains viscosity stability across temperature ranges while offering rapid water separation and minimal foaming.

## Specifications Summary
- **Base Oil Type:** Mineral
- **Density @ 15°C:** 0.845-0.855 g/ml (depending on grade)
- **Viscosity Index:** ≥95 (all grades)
- **Flash Point:** ≥210-215°C
- **Pour Point:** -6°C
- **Copper Corrosion:** 1a rating
- **Rust Protection:** Pass (ASTM D665)
- **Demulsibility:** 40/40/0 @ 54°C
- **Oxidation Stability:** Excellent

## Applications
Suitable for industrial hydraulic presses, injection molding equipment, construction machinery, forklifts, agricultural systems, and mining equipment with vane, piston, and gear-type pumps.

## Compliance
Meets IS 10522:1983 and Vickers/Denison performance standards.

# TECHNOFLUID GEAR EP 460 Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
"TECHNOFLUID GEAR EP 460 is a premium quality Extreme Pressure (EP) industrial gear lubricant" formulated with refined base oils and sulphur-phosphorus additives for severe-duty applications.

## Intended Uses
This lubricant addresses enclosed gear systems including spur, helical, bevel, and worm configurations across heavy industries like cement, steel, mining, and textiles.

## Compliance Standards
The product meets "AGMA 9005-F16 (EP)" and "DIN 51517 Part 3 (CLP)" along with ISO 12925-1 Type CKD and US Steel 224 requirements.

## Key Specifications
- Kinematic Viscosity @ 40°C: 410–460 cSt
- Flash Point: 230°C
- Pour Point: -6°C maximum
- Appearance: "Clear & Bright"
- Density @ 29.5°C: 0.88 g/cm³

## Performance Advantages
Notable capabilities include "Outstanding protection against wear and scuffing," superior thermal stability, and rapid water separation. The formulation supports extended drain intervals and reduces maintenance expenditures for industrial operations.

datasheets.rawText:
# TECHNOFLUID GEAR LUBE EP SERIES - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Gear Lube EP Series comprises high-performance industrial gear oils available in four viscosity grades (ISO VG 150, 220, 320, and 460).

## Key Characteristics
These oils are formulated with quality mineral base stocks and advanced EP additives to deliver "protection against wear, pitting, scuffing, and micropitting under heavy load" conditions.

## Primary Benefits
- Exceptional pressure and load-carrying performance
- Enhanced gear tooth protection against wear and surface fatigue
- Rapid water separation capabilities
- Robust rust and corrosion resistance
- Minimal foaming for reliable circulation
- Extended service intervals reducing operational disruptions

## Recommended Applications
The product serves enclosed industrial gearboxes featuring spur, helical, bevel, and worm gear configurations. It is well-suited for cement manufacturing, steel production, sugar processing, power generation, and mining operations.

## Specification Summary
All four viscosity grades share consistent baseline properties:
- Density: 0.860 g/ml at 15°C
- Flash Point: ≥210°C
- Pour Point: -6°C
- Copper Corrosion Rating: 1b
- Four Ball Weld Load: ≥315 kg
- FZG Load Stage: ≥12
- Demulsibility: 40/40/0 at 54°C

Kinematic viscosity at 40°C ranges from 150 cSt (VG 150) to 460 cSt (VG 460), with corresponding values at 100°C spanning 14.5 to 30.5 cSt.

# TECHNOFLUID CUTTING OIL - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Cutting Oil is a "high-performance metal working fluid formulated to provide effective lubrication, cooling, and surface protection during machining operations."

## Key Benefits
The fluid delivers "excellent lubricity reduces tool wear and improves surface finish of machined components" while providing effective heat management and rust protection for industrial machining.

## Applications
This cutting oil works across multiple machining processes—turning, milling, drilling, boring, grinding, tapping, and threading—on materials including mild steel, alloys, cast iron, and select non-ferrous metals.

## Typical Specifications
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Appearance (Concentrate) | Clear/Light Amber |
| Appearance (5% Emulsion) | Milky White |
| Kinematic Viscosity @ 40°C | 40–45 cSt |
| Density @ 15°C | 0.875 g/ml |
| Flash Point (COC) | ≥180°C |
| Pour Point | -6°C |
| pH (5% Emulsion) | 8.5–9.0 |

# TECHNOFLUID Industrial Engine Oils - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID manufactures high-performance diesel lubricants formulated for stationary and mobile industrial engines. The line includes multiple viscosity grades: SAE 15W-40 (API CI-4 Plus/CF-4/CH-4), SAE 20W-40, SAE 40, and SAE 50.

The oils feature "premium quality base oils and advanced additive technology" designed for heavy-duty protection. Notable characteristics include:

- **Detergency**: Maintains clean engines free from sludge buildup
- **Wear Protection**: Extends component lifespan through superior lubrication
- **Alkalinity**: High TBN (10-12 mg KOH/g for CI-4 Plus grade) neutralizes acidic combustion byproducts
- **Stability**: Good thermal and oxidation resistance for extended drain intervals

## Applications
Recommended for diesel generator sets, industrial engines, water pumps, compressors, construction equipment, and agricultural engines operating under continuous or heavy-load conditions.

The 15W-40 CI-4 Plus variant demonstrates:
- Density: 0.855 g/ml @ 29.5°C
- Viscosity Index: ≥130
- Flash Point: ≥220°C (COC)
- Pour Point: -21°C
- Meets foaming, wear, and corrosion resistance standards per ASTM testing

Key characteristics include "very low volatility reduces fluid loss and minimizes system fouling" and capability for "efficient heat transfer with consistent performance over extended service intervals." The product operates reliably "in systems operating at bulk temperatures up to 310°C."

## Key Performance Benefits
The fluid delivers "efficient and uniform heat transfer for precise temperature control" alongside "excellent resistance to thermal cracking and oxidation at elevated temperatures." It "minimizes sludge, varnish, and deposit formation inside heat transfer systems" while maintaining "stable viscosity over a wide operating temperature range."

## Applications
Recommended for textile, pharmaceutical, chemical, food processing, and plastic/rubber industries, specifically in "closed and open heat transfer systems."

## Operating Characteristics
Specifications include kinematic viscosity of 30 cSt at 40°C and 6 cSt at 100°C, with a viscosity index of 104. Flash point measures 210°C (COC), pour point -12°C, and auto-ignition temperature 380°C. Total acidity stands at 0.13 mg KOH/g.

# TECHNOFLUID Refrigeration Oil Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Refrigeration Oils are available in ISO VG 46 and 68 grades. These "premium-quality refrigeration compressor oils formulated from highly refined base oils with advanced additive technology" are designed for low-temperature refrigeration applications.

## Key Properties

**Viscosity & Temperature Performance:**
- ISO VG 46: 46 cSt @ 40°C, 6.6 cSt @ 100°C
- ISO VG 68: 68 cSt @ 40°C, 8.7 cSt @ 100°C
- Both grades maintain a viscosity index ≥ 95

**Thermal Characteristics:**
- Pour points: -30°C (VG 46) and -24°C (VG 68)
- Flash points: ≥210°C (VG 46) and ≥220°C (VG 68)

**Chemical Properties:**
- Density @ 15°C: 0.851 g/ml (VG 46), 0.858 g/ml (VG 68)
- Neutralization value: ≤ 0.03 mg KOH/g
- Moisture content: ≤ 50 ppm
- Ash content: ≤ 0.01% wt
- Sulphur content: Nil

**Compatibility:**
Compatible with CFC/HCFC refrigerants and suitable for reciprocating, rotary, and screw compressors per OEM specifications.

## Applications
Recommended for cold storage, ice plants, chillers, commercial and industrial refrigeration systems, and HVAC applications.

# TECHNOFLUID Quenching Oil Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
This document describes two quenching oil variants: **Q 39 (Group I)** and **QLP 32 (Group II)**, manufactured by Technofluid since 1971.

## Core Features
The oils are characterized by "advanced additives" and deliver "rapid heat extraction for improved hardness." Key strengths include oxidation stability, low volatility, and controlled cooling properties that minimize component distortion.

These products serve "general-purpose quenching operations" across manufacturing sectors, particularly for treating dies, springs, harrows, and automotive/agricultural components in heat treatment facilities and forging operations.

# TECHNOFLUID TRANSFORMER OIL - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID Transformer Oil is described as "a high-quality insulating mineral oil specially refined for use in electrical transformers and switchgear."

## Key Characteristics
The product offers "excellent dielectric strength, thermal stability, and oxidation resistance, ensuring reliable electrical insulation and efficient heat dissipation."

## Primary Applications
This insulating oil is recommended for power and distribution transformers, instrument transformers, oil-filled circuit breakers, and switchgear installations.

## Performance Standards
The product meets or exceeds IS 335:2018, IEC 60296, and ASTM D3487 specifications.

## Critical Specifications
- Breakdown Voltage: ≥60 Kv
- Moisture Content: ≤30 ppm
- Flash Point: ≥140°C

## Material Compatibility
The oil is "non-corrosive and compatible with transformer construction materials" with copper corrosion rating of 1a per ASTM D130.

# TECHNOFLUID EP GREASE SERIES - PRODUCT DATA SHEET

## Product Overview
TECHNOFLUID EP Grease Series comprises "high-performance extreme pressure greases formulated using high-quality mineral base oils and advanced EP additive technology." These products span NLGI grades 000 through 3, accommodating various lubrication needs from semi-fluid to stiff formulations.

The grease delivers several performance advantages: "Excellent extreme pressure and anti-wear properties protect components under heavy loads" and "Good mechanical stability ensures consistent grease structure under shear." Additional strengths include corrosion resistance and reduced maintenance requirements.

| 1     | 310–340           | 185             | -20 to 130      |

# TECHNOFLUID Lithium Complex Grease - Product Data Sheet

## Product Overview
"TECHNOFLUID Lithium Complex Grease is a premium high-temperature, heavy-duty grease formulated with lithium complex thickener and selected base oils."

## Key Features
The product delivers "superior mechanical stability and resistance to water washout" along with excellent performance under demanding conditions. It's engineered for applications requiring extended service intervals and component durability.

## Primary Applications
- Wheel bearings on disc brake vehicles
- High-speed and high-temperature bearing systems
- Industrial motors and machinery
- Steel, cement, and rolling mill operations
- Construction and mining equipment

## Temperature Performance
The three variants offer different operating ranges:
- RED GEL: -20 to 180°C
- BLUE GEL: -20 to 190°C
- 222 (Premium): -20 to 200°C

## Key Specifications
All variants maintain NLGI Grade 2 with worked penetration of 265-295 (0.1 mm). Drop points range from 235°C minimum (RED GEL) to 250°C (222 Premium), supporting high-temperature operation.

## Performance Properties
The grease demonstrates excellent load-carrying capacity, water resistance (≤2-3% loss), and "outstanding oxidation stability minimizes hardening and deposit formation."
